item.title: House again rebukes Trump on Iran, approving resolution to limit war powers
item.description: The House on Thursday voted to direct President Trump to end the war with Iran, after four Republicans joined Democrats to rebuke the president over his handling of the conflict.

item.title: Vance Boelter to be sentenced today in Minnesota lawmaker shootings
item.description: Vance Boelter, the man who admitted in court to shooting two Minnesota lawmakers and their spouses, is set to learn his sentence Thursday morning.

item.title: Trump threatens Iran and Houthis amid signs of U.S. military buildup
item.description: The U.S. moves more refueling planes to the Mideast and adds medics at a major military hospital as the war with Iran spreads to another key waterway.

item.title: FDA panel to consider loosening regulations for some peptides
item.description: The FDA panel will review seven peptides and decide if they should be added to a list of substances that can be safely produced by compounding pharmacies.

item.title: Can creditors renew a judgment against you if you still owe money?
item.description: Think an old debt judgment is about to expire? You may want to think again if you haven't paid the balance yet.

item.title: Can't afford a vet visit? Consider doing these 6 things
item.description: The cost of vet care keeps climbing, but skipping a visit isn't your only option when your budget runs short.

item.title: Judge to hear arguments on DOJ's subpoenas of The New York Times' journalists
item.description: A federal judge is set to hear arguments on the Justice Department's subpoenas of The New York Times journalists over reports about President Trump's Qatari-donated Air Force One. CBS News' Katrina Kaufman has the latest.

item.title: House passes resolution to limit Trump's war powers after 4 Republicans join Democrats on vote
item.description: The House passed a resolution to limit President Trump's war powers and direct him to end military operations in Iran after four Republicans joined Democrats on the vote. CBS News' Nikole Killion has more.

item.title: From the archives: Legendary late-night producer Peter Lassally
item.description: Peter Lassally, the fabled TV executive for such late-night hosts as Johnny Carson and David Letterman, and a mentor for many rising comedians, died July 19, 2026 at age 93. In this March 11, 2018 "Sunday Morning" profile, correspondent Mo Rocca talked with Lassally (affectionately dubbed "The Host Whisperer") about working with the kings of late-night. Lassally also talks about his childhood, part of which was spent in Nazi concentration camps, and also about whether he has had joy in his life.

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha moves off Louisiana, heads for Texas. See its path.
item.description: Tropical Storm Bertha is the second named storm of the Atlantic hurricane season.

item.title: Florida pastor alleges ChatGPT medical advice landed him in the hospital in OpenAI lawsuit
item.description: Florida pastor Scott Winters claims in his lawsuit against OpenAI that ChatGPT's poor medical recommendations sent him to the hospital. CBS News' Jo Ling Kent sat down with Winters to discuss his experience.

item.title: Member of Vance's Secret Service detail under investigation for leaking
item.description: The Secret Service said a member of Vice President JD Vance's detail is under investigation for "compromising operational and information security."

item.title: Malcolm-Jamal Warner's widow on actor's death: "My body went through shock"
item.description: Tenisha Warner, the widow of "The Cosby Show" actor Malcolm-Jamal Warner, spoke exclusively to "CBS Mornings" co-host Gayle King about coping with grief one year after his death.

item.title: Malcolm-Jamal Warner's widow says her body "went through shock" when her husband died
item.description: Tenisha Warner, the widow of "The Cosby Show" actor Malcolm-Jamal Warner, spoke exclusively to "CBS Mornings" co-host Gayle King about how she is coping one year after her husband's death.

item.title: 1.6 million eggs are recalled over potential salmonella contamination
item.description: The recall affects white-shell and cage-free eggs sold under multiple grocery brands in six states. No illnesses have been reported.

item.title: Can an employer fire you because of a wage garnishment?
item.description: Losing your job is the last thing you need when facing a wage garnishment. Here's what to know about the rules.

item.title: Aisha Tyler on decadelong "Criminal Minds" journey and how her role has impacted her real life
item.description: "Criminal Minds" star Aisha Tyler was only supposed to be in six episodes of the show, but her journey on the series has now spanned over a decade. She tells "CBS Mornings" how her role has impacted her in real life and what it was like to work with guest star Connor Storrie from "Heated Rivalry."

item.title: Former model scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein found dead in his Paris home
item.description: A former model who accused Jeffrey Epstein associate Daniel Siad of rape says his death in Paris is "very disappointing," as he never faced justice.

item.title: James Norton says playing a villain in "House of the Dragon" has "been really fun"
item.description: Actor James Norton describes what it is like to play a villain in "House of the Dragon," which is a prequel to "Game of Thrones" and reveals which scene made him nearly faint during shooting.

item.title: U.S. bolsters military in Middle East amid 12th night of strikes on Iran
item.description: The U.S. moved military assets, including B-1 bombers, into the Middle East amid the 12th straight night of airstrikes on Iran. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata and Aaron Navarro have the latest.

item.title: Global oil prices hit $100 a barrel amid attacks in Red Sea
item.description: The last time global oil prices settled above $100 was on May 22, data from FactSet shows.

item.title: Demand for unapproved peptides soars as influencers tout unproven health benefits
item.description: Doctors are wary as unapproved and under-researched peptides become more and more popular, driven by social media health trends. CBS News' Adam Yamaguchi reports.

item.title: Alleged California mailbox "fraud factory" hiding in plain sight, expert says
item.description: A CBS California Investigates report looked at how criminals can use P.O. mailboxes to intercept stolen and fabricated identities.

item.title: Democrats claim Social Security email included "misleading information"
item.description: Lawmakers claim Commissioner Frank Bisignano used an agency email to exaggerate tax savings under the Republicans' OBBBA while inserting partisan political messaging.

item.title: U.S. and Saudis sign nuclear deal, contingent on joining Abraham Accords
item.description: President Trump said on Thursday the deal would include no enrichment of nuclear material.

item.title: Man shot and killed by police in Madison, Wisconsin, sparking protests
item.description: Madison's chief of police says an officer in Wisconsin's capital city shot and killed a man who had injured him with a knife while resisting arrest.

item.title: Navy withholding sailors' service history, in another curb on press access
item.description: The U.S. Navy is taking a major step to restrict press access to information about service members by no longer releasing information about any sailor's service history.

item.title: Why Smart People Fall for Scams | Money Moves with Jill Schlesinger
item.description: Jill and Mark discuss a $50 million Ponzi scheme that ensnared dozens of investors with promises of big, risk-free returns. Then, they break down the emotional manipulation tactics scammers use to gain your trust and how to protect yourself as AI makes fraud more sophisticated and harder to spot than ever. Plus, a burned-out caller is desperate for a career reset and wonders how long she can afford to coast on her savings before figuring out her next move.

item.title: Google fined $1 billion by EU over its Play app store and search engine
item.description: The EU fined Google 890 million euros ($1 billion) after it said the tech giant broke digital antitrust regulations by setting up Google Play and its search engine to steer consumers toward its own services and apps over others'.

item.title: ICE detentions hit highest monthly total in June since crackdown began
item.description: More than 43,000 immigrants were arrested last month as DHS continues to detain people in less-publicized enforcement surges.
